items.safeLabel: Inspired by George Peng Temperature Control
items.sourceAttribution: World Brewers Cup 2025 champion reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.fallbackReason: Solo Dripper is not currently in catalog; OREA flat-bottom fallback keeps the preset safe inside supported equipment.
items.visibleSummary: Temperature-decline competition style adapted to a supported flat-bottom brewer.
internalTips: Use bloom and early water as the hotter extraction phase, then keep later pulses calmer to preserve floral clarity.
Treat the single temperature field as service-safe midpoint; do not imply multi-kettle precision unless user controls it.
Flat-bottom fallback should stay low-agitation and avoid wall chasing.
guardrails: Keep temperature within method bounds and explain this as inspired guidance, not a locked reproduction.
Fallback brewer must remain visible when original brewer is unsupported.

items.safeLabel: Inspired by Martin Woelfl OREA V4
items.sourceAttribution: World Brewers Cup 2024 winning pour-over reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Fast flat-bottom four-pour style for clean, sweet competition balance.
internalTips: Keep four fast, centered pulses and protect a level bed.
Use low kettle height and compact circles because OREA-style fast bottoms punish uneven flooding.
Target clean finish before chasing heavier body.
guardrails: Do not exceed flat-bottom capacity or force late agitation when drawdown slows.
Keep this as an inspired service prefill and still let AI Brew guardrails adapt to the bean.

items.sourceAttribution: World Brewers Cup 2023 champion Origami reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Origami five-equal-pour style for fruit clarity and controlled sweetness.
internalTips: Hold five near-equal pours so the planner creates repeatable cumulative volumes.
Keep Origami cone mode clean and avoid high late agitation.
Use sweetness and tropical fruit clarity as direction, not certainty.
guardrails: Keep equal-pour timing inside normal pour-over service windows.
Do not claim the preset reproduces a competition routine exactly.

items.sourceAttribution: World Brewers Cup 2016 champion 4:6 method reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: 4:6-inspired five-pour V60 prefill for sweetness and body control.
internalTips: Use the first two pours to steer sweetness and acidity, then the last three to manage body.
Keep all five pours positive and rounded to service-friendly cumulative targets.
If drawdown slows, protect sweetness by reducing agitation rather than pushing finer.
guardrails: Keep 4:6 as a safe inspired pattern and let bean, grinder, and water guardrails override.
Do not present the result as exact competition replication.

items.safeLabel: Inspired by Matt Winton Five-Pour V60
items.sourceAttribution: World Brewers Cup champion-style V60 reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Five-pour V60 competition style for repeatable clarity and balance.
internalTips: Use five clean pulses with modest agitation and stable intervals.
Bias toward even extraction rather than extreme acidity or body.
Keep final pour calm to reduce dryness risk.
guardrails: Do not force five pours if the selected brewer becomes unsupported.
Use drawdown feedback before recommending finer grind.

items.sourceAttribution: Nordic competition-style light roast V60 reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Nordic light-roast V60 style for floral clarity with restrained finish agitation.
internalTips: Use higher but safe temperature and even saturation for dense light roasts.
Keep the final phase calm so clarity does not turn dry.
Treat aromatics as volatile and protect them with clean bed geometry.
guardrails: Reduce temperature if roast is medium-dark or water buffer is low.
Do not infer Nordic flavor profile without bean evidence.

items.sourceAttribution: Flat-bottom World Brewers Cup style reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Flat-bottom competition balance with a controlled, level-bed four-pour cadence.
internalTips: Front-load enough water to build sweetness while keeping bed height stable.
Use centered flat-bed pours and avoid edge flooding.
Let balance and clean finish override aggressive extraction pushes.
guardrails: Keep bed level and avoid last-second swirl on flat-bottom brewers.
Do not claim any specific champion recipe ownership.

items.sourceAttribution: Championship-era high-clarity V60 reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: High-clarity V60 style with even saturation and controlled extraction pressure.
internalTips: Prioritize even wetting, stable flow, and clean finish before pushing finer grind.
Use continuous or near-continuous middle loading when flow remains healthy.
Treat clarity as direction, not a promise.
guardrails: Keep agitation bounded when grinder has high-fines risk.
Require cup feedback before stronger extraction changes.

items.sourceAttribution: Chemex clean-cup competition reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Chemex clean-cup style for tea-like body and low sediment.
internalTips: Rinse thick paper hard and protect the vent side.
Use wider service time windows because Chemex drawdown is slower.
Keep pour path center-to-mid and avoid paper-wall pouring.
guardrails: Respect Chemex minimum service time and capacity.
Do not chase ultra-fast drawdown with excessive agitation.

items.sourceAttribution: General cone-dripper competition approach, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Cone dripper competition-style prefill with front-loaded sweetness and clean finish.
internalTips: Use a strong bloom and middle load, then calm final pour.
Keep the cone bed centered and avoid bypass-heavy wall pours.
Choose balance unless user targets a specific sensory axis.
guardrails: Do not overfit to a named barista when the preset is a synthesized style.
Let brewer geometry and grinder guardrails override the cadence.

items.sourceAttribution: Global educator V60 reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Modern balanced V60 prefill with even saturation and stable drawdown.
internalTips: Prioritize even bloom and a stable middle pour before final settling.
Use high-but-safe temperature only when roast and water support it.
Keep the final bed flat rather than adding aggressive late motion.
guardrails: Adapt to roast level and water buffer before raising temperature.
Do not present as an exact creator-owned recipe.

items.sourceAttribution: Global educator one-cup V60 reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: One-cup V60 style with repeatable small pulses and clean finish.
internalTips: Use repeatable pulse volumes and avoid flooding a small cone.
Keep agitation useful but bounded for home grinder variability.
Default to balance unless bean metadata supports a stronger target.
guardrails: Avoid excessive late swirl with high-fines grinders.
Use source attribution as reference only.

items.sourceAttribution: Global educator AeroPress reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Clean AeroPress immersion style with measured steep and gentle press.
internalTips: Use immersion timing and press rate as the main extraction levers.
Avoid over-stirring fine grinds because AeroPress can become harsh quickly.
Keep bypass optional and only if the target ratio requires it.
guardrails: Respect chamber capacity and press safety.
Do not convert AeroPress guidance into pour-over pulses.

items.sourceAttribution: High-extraction pour-over education reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: High-extraction V60 style with evenness-first pouring and feedback guardrails.
internalTips: Increase extraction through even saturation and flow health before using finer grind.
Keep agitation controlled so high extraction does not become dryness.
Use drawdown and taste feedback as stop conditions.
guardrails: High extraction is a direction, not a certainty.
Do not recommend finer grind if drawdown or bitterness risk is already high.

items.sourceAttribution: Modern two-pour V60 education reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Two-pour V60 style with a long bloom and one main extraction pour.
internalTips: Planner should emit two positive pours even though the UI count options start at three.
Use a full bloom and one controlled extraction pour, then let drawdown finish cleanly.
Keep the second pour calm if grinder fines are high.
guardrails: Keep two-pour timing within safe pour-over service time.
Do not shorten so far that bloom or drawdown guidance becomes unsafe.

items.sourceAttribution: April Coffee low-agitation brewer reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: April-style low-agitation four-pour prefill for fast, clean service.
internalTips: Use short pulses and quick kettle resets.
Keep agitation low and avoid stretching the final phase.
When using V60 fallback, preserve low-agitation intent rather than forcing flat-bottom behavior.
guardrails: Use supported brewer geometry and capacity checks.
Do not make low-agitation recipes too weak; keep target ratio bounded.

items.sourceAttribution: Nordic AeroPress education reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Nordic AeroPress style for clean aromatics and light body.
internalTips: Use clean immersion, gentle agitation, and slow press as primary controls.
Protect light-roast aromatics with moderate temperature rather than harsh stirring.
Keep recipe flexible for dose and chamber capacity.
guardrails: Do not overfill AeroPress chamber.
Do not imply Nordic cup character without bean evidence.

items.sourceAttribution: Roaster brew-guide V60 reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Roaster-style V60 prefill for fruit-forward balance and clean sweetness.
internalTips: Use a clear bloom and middle load to build sweetness.
Keep final pour calm for a clean finish.
Let process and roast metadata decide whether fruit-forward target remains appropriate.
guardrails: Use target as a preference, not a promised cup result.
Keep adjustments inside AI Brew safety bounds.

items.sourceAttribution: Roaster Chemex brew-guide reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Chemex cafe-classic style for clean body and rounded sweetness.
internalTips: Respect thick-paper flow and longer drawdown.
Use gentle center-to-mid pours to avoid vent blockage.
Keep body soft rather than heavy or silty.
guardrails: Do not pour down the paper wall.
Keep Chemex service timing and dose capacity realistic.

items.sourceAttribution: Kyoto brew-guide V60 reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Kyoto V60 style using measured pulse control and sweetness-first balance.
internalTips: Use 4:6-inspired pulse logic without claiming exact creator ownership.
Keep first two pours sensory-directional and later pours body-directional.
Use clean drawdown feedback before changing grind.
guardrails: Keep source attribution clear and safe.
Do not overfit the method when bean metadata conflicts.

items.sourceAttribution: Roaster Origami brew-guide reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Origami roaster-style prefill for fruit clarity with a controlled four-pour cadence.
internalTips: Use cone mode as the default when the user wants fruit clarity and a clean finish.
Front-load sweetness, then keep the final pour calm to reduce dryness.
If the user switches to wave filter, keep the bed level and reduce edge flow.
guardrails: Do not imply this is an official Rogue Wave recipe inside the app.
Let Origami filter style and bean metadata override the generic fruit-forward target.

items.sourceAttribution: Modern roaster pour-over approach, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Modern light-roast pour-over prefill for aromatics and transparent sweetness.
internalTips: Use even saturation and restrained final agitation for high-aroma coffees.
Protect delicate florals by adjusting water and temperature before forcing finer grind.
Use stronger extraction pressure only when drawdown remains healthy.
guardrails: Do not attach specific roaster tasting notes unless the user provides the bean.
Do not promise floral output; keep it as target direction.

items.sourceAttribution: Nordic roaster filter-brewing reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Nordic roaster-style filter prefill for clean sweetness and balanced acidity.
internalTips: Use balanced mineral water and moderate-high temperature for clean specialty filter.
Keep the middle load stable so sweetness does not collapse into thin acidity.
Prefer cup feedback over large ratio jumps.
guardrails: Do not claim cafe-specific calibration without grinder and water evidence.
Keep target changes small and reversible.

items.sourceAttribution: Hario Switch hybrid immersion-percolation references, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Switch hybrid prefill for immersion sweetness with clean percolation finish.
internalTips: Use the Switch planner's valve and chamber safety as the source of truth.
Favor immersion sweetness, then release cleanly instead of adding aggressive late agitation.
If the selected Switch size is too small, let the exact-size guardrail adjust or block.
guardrails: Never bypass Switch chamber safety limits.
Do not translate this into V60-only pour language in the public guide.

items.sourceAttribution: Clever Dripper immersion reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Clever immersion-first prefill for easy sweetness and forgiving extraction.
internalTips: Use steep time and drawdown cleanliness as the main controls.
Keep agitation minimal after wetting so fines settle before release.
If the cup tastes hollow, extend steep before changing dose.
guardrails: Do not produce pour-over pulse instructions for Clever.
Keep release and drawdown steps clear in the guide.

items.sourceAttribution: Global educator French press reference, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: French press clarity prefill with long steep, gentle press, and clean decant.
internalTips: Use time, skimming, and decanting to improve clarity while preserving body.
Do not over-press or disturb settled fines.
For dark roasts, lower temperature before changing ratio.
guardrails: Keep French press guide immersion-specific.
Do not promise sediment-free results.

items.sourceAttribution: Modern moka pot low-heat brew-guide references, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Moka pot prefill for low-heat, steady-flow concentrated coffee.
internalTips: Use heat control and stop-before-sputter as primary extraction guardrails.
Keep the output concentrated; do not translate moka into filter-coffee ratio expectations.
If bitterness risk is high, reduce heat and temperature before changing dose.

Do not overstate espresso-like pressure or crema.

items.sourceAttribution: Japanese iced V60 flash-brew references, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Japanese iced V60 prefill that brews hot concentrate directly over measured ice.
internalTips: Keep hot-water and ice split explicit in every guide.
Use slightly finer grind and strong bloom only inside iced guardrails.
Do not add late bypass water after the hot concentrate plan is set.
guardrails: Never hide ice dilution math.
Do not call final beverage ratio the same as hot extraction ratio.

items.sourceAttribution: Siphon/vacuum brewer guide references, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Siphon prefill for clean aromatics with controlled heat and gentle agitation.
internalTips: Use heat stability and gentle agitation as primary controls.
Protect aromatics by avoiding violent stirring or overlong drawdown.
Keep safety notes clear because siphon uses heat and glass.
guardrails: Do not produce pour-over pulse language for siphon.
Keep flame/heat safety and drawdown clarity visible in guide steps.

items.sourceAttribution: Cold brew immersion system references, adapted as safe planner prefill.
items.visibleSummary: Cold brew immersion prefill for smooth concentrate-style brewing and clean filtration.
internalTips: Use long steep and clean filtration as the main brew controls.
Keep serving dilution separate from extraction planning.
Warn that cold brew mutes some volatile florals compared with hot flash brew.
guardrails: Do not label cold brew as hot extraction even if the shared form field uses brewMode.
Do not promise low acidity as a certain outcome.

items.sourceAttribution: Internal taste-target preset synthesized from safe pour-over controls.
items.visibleSummary: Taste-target prefill for more sweetness with controlled front-loaded extraction.
internalTips: Use bloom and middle load to increase sweetness perception without harsh late agitation.
Prefer balanced water buffer and moderate temperature.
If cup turns flat, shift target rather than forcing stronger extraction.
guardrails: Taste targets are preferences, not certainty.
Keep recipe changes small until cup feedback exists.

items.sourceAttribution: Internal taste-target preset synthesized from safe pour-over controls.
items.visibleSummary: Taste-target prefill for brighter acidity with a safe shorter cadence.
internalTips: Use slightly shorter contact and calmer final pour to preserve brightness.
Avoid making the recipe sour by checking sweetness and drawdown.
Use lower buffer water only within safe mineral guidance.
guardrails: Do not shorten below safe manual-filter service time.
If acidity reads sour, restore sweetness rather than lowering extraction further.

items.sourceAttribution: Internal taste-target preset synthesized from safe flat-bottom and cone controls.
items.visibleSummary: Taste-target prefill for more body with controlled sweetness and texture.
internalTips: Use slightly tighter ratio and middle loading to build texture.
Prefer flat-bottom brewer when selected; keep V60 fallback centered.
Watch bitterness risk on dark roast and decaf.
guardrails: Do not chase body through muddy late agitation.
Respect roast and process bitterness risks.

items.sourceAttribution: Internal taste-target preset synthesized from safe clarity-first pour-over controls.
items.visibleSummary: Taste-target prefill for clarity with even saturation and restrained finish.
internalTips: Use even extraction and low late agitation to reduce muddiness.
Protect aromatics with stable flow and no wall chasing.
If body becomes too light, shift toward balance instead of over-extracting.
guardrails: Clarity target must still obey grinder fines and process variability guardrails.
Do not promise transparent cup outcome.

items.sourceAttribution: Internal fast-service preset synthesized from safe manual-filter constraints.
items.visibleSummary: Fast manual-filter prefill that shortens service while keeping safe extraction windows.
internalTips: Shorten contact through three controlled pours and a clean drawdown target.
Keep minimum service time above unsafe ultra-short filter extraction.
Use fast drawdown as workflow goal, not as flavor certainty.
guardrails: Do not let total time drop below safe pour-over minimum.
If cup is sour or thin, return toward balance before changing grinder aggressively.
